name: King of August
classification_notes: A single-weight connected script with elegant letterforms and swash alternates, consistent with formal or calligraphic script tradition.
summary: A single-weight formal script by Dušan Jelesijević, released in 2022, with swash alternates suited to product labels and short-form display work.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and published in April 2022, this script carries a refined, flowing quality that suits packaging, product names and title treatments. The family ships as a single Regular style with 404 glyphs, standard ligatures and OpenType swash variants for selected capitals. Extended Latin coverage spans 102 languages.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe King of August as a simple, elegant script with power to fit and lead any project, positioning it as ideal for product names, labels, packages, titles and shorter paragraphs. It includes swash alternates as an OpenType feature.

name: Ironia
classification_notes: The foundry classifies it as Display and describes it as a modern blackletter evoking a medieval archaic impression, placing it squarely in the blackletter category with a contemporary approach.
summary: A single-weight modern blackletter by Dušan Jelesijević, evoking a medieval visual character through contemporary letterform construction.
description: Released in July 2025, Ironia offers one Regular style with 370 glyphs and coverage across 102 languages in the Extended Latin range. The designer cites no direct historical references, yet the typeface carries a clear archaic quality closest to Middle Age lettering. With no OpenType features, it is a straightforward display face suited to editorial headings, packaging and branding where a gothic tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Ironia as a modern blackletter available in a single Regular style, giving words an archaic visual impression closest to the Middle Ages. The typeface covers an Extended Latin language set with no OpenType features, designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in July 2025.

name: Metropola
classification_notes: Metropola draws on Victorian-era display typefaces with expressive, high-contrast letterforms suited primarily to headlines and titling work. The foundry classifies it explicitly as Display.
summary: A Victorian-inspired display family in four weights with a variable axis, covering extended Latin and Cyrillic with swash alternates and oldstyle figures.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in April 2021, Metropola takes its cues from Victorian-era display lettering, carrying that genre's expressive weight contrast into a contemporary four-weight family with a corresponding variable version. Each style ships with dingbats and OpenType features including swashes, oldstyle numerals, ligatures and fractions. Extended Latin across 102 languages and a full Cyrillic set broaden its utility beyond purely English-language work.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Metropola as a font family with four classic weights and a variable version for those with specific visual appetite, inspired by Victorian-era typefaces. They note its strong display characteristics while acknowledging it can function in longer text settings, and highlight its extended Latin character map, Cyrillic support, dingbats and OpenType features including swashes, oldstyle numerals, ligatures and fractions.

name: Landsick
classification_notes: Classified as Script by the foundry; a single-weight casual handwritten script with lively, informal letterforms suited to display and branding use.
summary: A single-weight casual script by Dušan Jelesijević, released in 2018 with extended Latin coverage across 102 languages.
description: A lively handwritten script in one weight, designed by Dušan Jelesijević and first released in March 2018. The 370-glyph set covers extended Latin for 102 languages and includes standard ligatures. Its informal, energetic character makes it suitable for branding, packaging and display contexts where a handcrafted feel is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Landsick as a single-weight script family that is charming, characteristic, lively and playful, and ready to be used everywhere.

name: Horrorama
classification_notes: Classified by the foundry as Hand-made; the typeface uses expressive brush strokes with an irregular, hand-drawn quality suited to display and decorative use.
summary: A single-weight brush display face by Dušan Jelesijević with extended Latin and Cyrillic support, designed for expressive, hand-made aesthetics.
description: Released in 2009 by Tour de Force, Horrorama is a brush-drawn display typeface with an intentionally rough, hand-made character. It covers 384 glyphs across extended Latin and Cyrillic scripts, supporting over 100 languages. Ligature support and its loose, expressive strokes make it best suited to horror-themed, editorial or decorative contexts.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Horrorama as coming from the same creative direction as Punkerro Crust and Rough the Type, presenting it as a special typeface for lovers of brush movement, designed by Dušan Jelesijević and first released in August 2009.

name: Selektor
classification_notes: The foundry classifies it as a geometrical sans, inspired by technical lettering. Its geometric construction with characteristic letter endings places it firmly in the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A geometric sans from Dušan Jelesijević with a faint technical character, offered in three weights with matching italics.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in June 2013, Selektor draws from the tradition of technical lettering, lending a few of its characters subtle distinctive terminals that prevent it from reading as entirely neutral. Three weights — Light, Regular and Bold — each come with a matching italic, and all six styles include small caps, fractions and standard ligatures. Coverage extends to 102 languages across Extended Latin, making it broadly applicable for both continuous text and display settings.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Selektor as a small geometric sans-serif family inspired by technical letters, with specific letter endings that give it a peculiar impression. They position it as neutral, corporate and well balanced without being cold, and note that it works across long-form paragraphs, titles and product names. The family ships in three weights with matching italics, small caps, fractions and additional OpenType features.

name: Dietal
classification_notes: Classified explicitly as slab serif by the foundry; the condensed proportions and mixture of western, calligraphic and display influences sit firmly within the slab category despite the eclectic stylistic range.
summary: A condensed slab serif in five weights, available in curved and sharp joining variants, blending western, calligraphic and display influences.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in July 2019, Dietal spans ten styles across two subfamilies: the standard curved version and a sharp-joining counterpart. The family draws on a wide range of influences from sans and slab through to calligraphy and western display, giving it a versatility unusual for a condensed face. Extended Latin and Cyrillic coverage across 463 glyphs makes it well suited to multilingual display and editorial work.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Dietal as a condensed slab serif family available in two versions with curved and sharp joining, spanning five weights and mixing elements from sans, slab, calligraphy, western and display categories. It includes stylistic alternates, ordinals and tabular figures as OpenType features, with extended Latin and Cyrillic character support.

name: Blond
classification_notes: A clean, modern sans-serif with uniform stroke contrast and a geometric sensibility. The foundry classifies it simply as Sans-Serif, and its even weight distribution across 11 weights points to a geometric or neo-grotesque approach.
summary: A uniform modern sans-serif in 11 weights with matching italics, covering extended Latin across 102 languages.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in June 2018, Blond offers 22 styles running from Thin to Heavy with matching italics. The family includes a small stylistic set affecting the 'a' and 'g', standard ligatures, and coverage of 102 languages via an extended Latin character map. Its even weight distribution and consistent rhythm make it serviceable across editorial and screen contexts.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Blond as a modern sans-serif family of 22 members, offering 11 weights from Thin to Heavy with matching italics. They note it is distinctive, recognisable and uniform, calling it a well-balanced typeface suited to any situation from editorial to web design, with extended Latin character support and a small stylistic set.

name: Connectica
classification_notes: A monolinear joined script, meaning all letterforms connect in a continuous flow. The foundry classifies it as Script, and its monoline construction distinguishes it from calligraphic or brush scripts.
summary: A joined monolinear script in two weights with OpenType initial forms for capital letters that lack a left-side joining stroke.
description: Designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released in June 2019, Connectica is a monoline connected script built around continuous joining strokes between characters. Capital letters such as E, F, U, V, W and Y use a swash initial form via OpenType to handle the absence of a left-side entry stroke cleanly. The family covers 102 languages across Extended Latin and includes standard ligatures alongside the initial forms feature.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Connectica as a joined monolinear script family, noting that the name reflects its connected nature. The design comes in Light and Regular weights, and a fake Swash OpenType feature handles initial capitals where letters like E, F, U, V, W and Y cannot carry a joining line on their left side.

name: Kamenica Texture
classification_notes: Classified as Display by the foundry; a decorative extension of the Kamenica family featuring six textured styles intended for ornamental and headline use rather than text setting.
summary: A decorative, textured extension of the Kamenica family by Dušan Jelesijević, offered in six distressed style variants for display use.
description: Released in 2017, this family applies six distinct texture treatments to the Kamenica letterforms, producing worn and layered effects suited to poster, packaging and branding work. Each style carries a different degree or character of texture, from subtle grain to heavier distress. Extended Latin coverage spans 102 languages, broadening its practical reach beyond purely ornamental applications.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Kamenica Texture as a decorative extension of the Kamenica font family, containing six textured styles and classified as Display, with Extended Latin language support and standard ligature OpenType features.

name: Brisko Display
classification_notes: The foundry classifies this as Display; it is derived from Brisko Sans Black, a single heavy-weight display cut intended for large-scale typesetting rather than text use.
summary: A single-style display cut derived from Brisko Sans Black, designed by Dušan Jelesijević for large-scale headline use.
description: Drawn from the Black weight of the Brisko Sans family, this display variant is offered in one style only, pitched at poster and headline work. Extended Latin coverage spans 102 languages, and OpenType support includes standard ligatures. Released in 2013 and updated to version 2, it is a focused tool rather than a versatile family.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Brisko Display as an alternate version of Brisko Sans Black, classified as Display, with a single Regular style and Extended Latin language support covering 102 languages. It was designed by Dušan Jelesijević and released on 1 January 2013.

name: Jeles
classification_notes: A high-contrast serif with round slab serifs, ball terminals and soft corners, designed primarily for display use. The strong thick-thin contrast and decorative detailing place it firmly in the display category despite readable text settings.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with round slab serifs and ball terminals, suited to fashion, cosmetics and editorial headlines.
description: Designed by Slobodan Jelesijević and released in 2015, Jeles draws on classic high-contrast serif traditions and updates them with soft corners and rounded slab serifs for a contemporary feel. The family spans seven styles across Normal and Condensed widths, with the upright cuts carrying authority for headlines and the italics lending a livelier character to subheadings. Its sturdy thin strokes make it practical for both screen and print, and the decorative quality suits branding work in fashion, jewellery and food.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Jeles as inheriting the beauty of old type classics from this genre, blended with an elegant modern approach featuring soft corners, round slab serifs and tasty ball terminals. Designed primarily for display use, the family spans seven styles in Condensed and Normal widths, with the condensed cuts offered in two weights each with italics, and the normal styles running from Regular through Bold to Black. They recommend the full family for maximum diversity and flexibility, noting its suitability for editorial design, cosmetics, fashion, food and jewellery, as well as web banners, apps and e-books.

name: Poruka
classification_notes: A slanted, connected script with monoline letterforms and a gently condensed proportion, designed for headline and display use rather than body text.
summary: A slanted connected script by Dušan Jelesijević with monoline letterforms, designed for headlines, labels and short display texts.
description: Built on monoline letter construction with a gently condensed silhouette, Poruka sits between casual handwriting and formal script without leaning too far into either. Two Stylistic Sets offer uppercase letters spanning full font height and handwritten alternates for 'b', 'd', 'h' and 'l', while Discretionary Ligatures add graphic word-forms for terms like 'yes', 'no' and 'ciao'. Suited to invitations, packaging, posters and editorial applications where a script needs to hold its own against heavier visual surroundings.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Poruka as a slanted connected script with a gently condensed look and monoline letterforms designed with elegance and stylistic uniformity. They position it for shorter texts and headlines, noting it works equally well as a webfont and in print across invitations, labels, packages, posters and editorial contexts.

name: Oceanis
classification_notes: Explicitly described as neo-grotesque, following the tradition of Helvetica and Arial, with low contrast and neutral character — hallmarks of the neo-grotesque genre.
summary: A ten-weight neo-grotesque family by Dušan Jelesijević, modelled on the Helvetica and Arial tradition with Cyrillic support added.
description: Designed with the neutral, compact character of classic neo-grotesques, Oceanis spans ten weights from Hairline to Black with low contrast throughout. The family covers Extended Latin across 102 languages as well as Cyrillic, and ships with OpenType features including standard ligatures, fractions, tabular figures, superscript and ordinals. It positions itself as a general-purpose workhorse rather than a distinctive voice.
foundry_blurb: Tour de Force describe Oceanis as a neo-grotesque family crafted in the tradition of Helvetica and Arial, intended as a working horse font that fits into every project. Neutral, compact and low-contrasted, it spans ten weights from Hairline to Black with Extended Latin and Cyrillic language support, plus a set of OpenType features including ligatures, fractions, tabular figures, superscript and subscript.
